subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with recognizable introduction, methodology, analysis, interpretation and reflection sections
Deliberate organization evidenced by a table of contents, numbered sections and labelled tables/figures
Relevant content that stays mostly focused on the research question
weaknesses: Abrupt transitions between statistical tests and occasional repetition undermine coherence
Duplicated headings and inconsistent referencing interrupt seamless flow
Long narrative passages and generic definitions create some irrelevance and redundancy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and logical development at band 2 (basic flow but fragmented), organization at band 3 (clear sections but minor flaws) and conciseness at band 2 (mostly relevant with unnecessary elements). Averaging these and balancing the overall quality yields a score of 2 out of 4 for Presentation, reflecting basic logical flow and adequate structure but noticeable issues in conciseness and coherence.

strengths: Use of key symbols (r, Hâ, xÌ) and standard formulae demonstrates some command of mathematical language
Well-structured variable table clearly identifies independent and dependent variables
Effective scatterplot representation aids interpretation of correlation assumptions
weaknesses: Notation is often inconsistent or inaccurate (e.g., âvâ instead of y, Chi-square as âxÂ²â)
Limited variety of representations and poor formatting in some figures (clipped labels, missing axis scales)
Terminology slips and typographical errors create occasional confusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (language and notation, multiple representations, clarity and consistency) are ranked band 2âsome correct use but frequent inconsistencies and limited representations. Thus a score of 2 out of 4 accurately reflects the studentâs mostly appropriate mathematical communication with room for greater precision and consistency.

strengths: Topic choice is clearly personally motivated, showing genuine initial engagement
Use of multiple standard tests (Pearson, Spearman, ANOVA) indicates some initiative
weaknesses: Little evidence of original strategy or adaptation beyond textbook procedures
Grouping into regions is generic with no statistical or literature-based justification
Analysis is largely formulaic with minimal personal questioning or deeper exploration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand (independent thinking, personal approach, creativity and initiative) aligns with band 1âlimited independent thinking and a formulaic approach despite clear motivation. A score of 1 out of 3 reflects this modest level of personal engagement.

strengths: Acknowledges surprising results and data limitations, demonstrating basic reflective awareness
Identifies that initial expectations were challenged, indicating some connection to learning
weaknesses: Reflection is brief and largely descriptive, lacking depth or probing critique
Critical analysis of statistical choices and assumptions remains superficial
Links between mathematical findings and personal understanding are sparse
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With depth of reflection, critical analysis and connection to understanding all at band 1 (limited, basic and sparse), the Reflection criterion merits 1 out of 3, reflecting a need for deeper, more critical and better connected reflection.

strengths: Application of relevant SL statistical techniques (Pearson, Spearman, Chi-square, ANOVA) shows solid course-level use
Clear substitution into formulas (e.g., Pearson sums) and correct expected frequency calculations demonstrate procedural rigor
Use of technology for ANOVA p-value showcases effective integration of tools
weaknesses: Several computational and notation errors (miswritten Pearson denominator, typos in log tables) reduce accuracy
Some hypotheses lack precision and rounding is unjustified in places
Omission of p-value checks and explicit sample sizes in ANOVA limit completeness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands show relevance at band 3, accuracy at band 2 and understanding at band 3. This mixture of solid course-level application, partial correctness and good conceptual grasp supports a score of 3 out of 6 for Use of Mathematics; it balances the demonstration of multiple techniques with noted errors and omissions.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choice of topic is personally motivated, yet the mathematical pathway closely follows textbook procedures without adaptation.  There is **little evidence of the student devising original statistical strategy or questioning assumptions**, matching âlimited evidence of independent thinking,â band 1.

Explanations of what each statistic measures show **sound conceptual grasp**; for example, the student interprets r = 0.215 using a guideline table and comments on causality.  Misinterpretations do appear, but understanding is good in sections and extends across multiple toolsâconsistent with âgood understanding in parts,â band 3.

Some calculations are carried out correctly (scatter-plot regression, Pearson arithmetic), but others contain errors or unjustified rounding (e.g., incorrect log-table, mis-written ANOVA sums).  Hence the work is **partially correct overall**, fitting band 2.

Most content relates to the research question, yet long narrative passages (e.g., personal anecdotes repeated in Reflection and Introduction) and extensive quotation of generic definitions add **noticeable but not overwhelming irrelevance**.  Redundancy in explaining each statistical formula also inflates length.  Thus the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â band 2.

Symbols such as \(r, H_0, \bar{x}\) and formulae are used, but notation is **frequently inaccurate or inconsistent** (e.g., mis-placed bars, âvâ instead of \(y\), Chi-square written âx^2â).  Descriptive words like âAvonaâ and âpersonsâ show limited command of mathematical language.  Hence âsome appropriate mathematical language and notationâ â band 2.

Explanations usually follow each calculation, allowing the reader to see where numbers come from, but **terminology slips and typographical errors** (e.g., âpoverty \(\sim 101\) cccccoâ) create inconsistency.  Overall clarity is fair but not sustained, so âmostly clear with some consistency,â band 2.

Pearson, Spearman, Chi-square and one-way ANOVA are all **relevant SL statistical techniques**, applied to address the research question.  Although the dataset is small, the mathematics is firmly at course level and not trivial, satisfying band 3 (relevant mathematics at course level used).

The exploration contains tables, a single scatterplot and a trend-line; these representations are **appropriate but few** and some are poorly formatted (figure labels clipped, no axis scales in appendix tables).  There is no visual of the ANOVA or Chi-square contingency table beyond raw numbers.  Thus usage meets âsome appropriate representations,â band 2.

The reflection section voices surprise at a weak correlation and acknowledges data limitations, yet discussion is **brief and largely descriptive**.  It does not probe why statistical results conflict or explore methodological improvements in depth.  This aligns with âlimited reflection,â band 1.

Employing a variety of standard tests (Pearson, Spearman, ANOVA) shows initiative, but execution follows formulaic steps copied from online sources and contains computational errors.  **Creativity in modelling or data treatment is minimal**, so band 1 is appropriate.

The student follows a recognizable sequenceâintroduction, background, methodology, analysis, interpretation and reflectionâwhich provides **basic logical flow**.  However, transitions between statistical tests are abrupt and results are sometimes repeated or contradicted, so the line of argument is **often fragmented** (e.g., concluding Pearson gives a âweak positiveâ then immediately calling it âmoderateâ).  Consequently, coherence is present but the logical development remains only partial, aligning with band 2.

Critical analysis is restricted to stating that other factors âmayâ influence crime and that government data could be biased.  There is **no rigorous evaluation of the statistical choices, assumptions or effect sizes**, corresponding to basic analysis with limited critiqueâband 1.

The student recognises that initial expectations were challenged and claims improved understanding, but **explicit links between mathematical findings and personal mathematical learning are sparse**.  This shows only a limited connection to understanding, band 1.

A clear table of contents, numbered sections and labelled tables/figures show **deliberate organisation**.  Introduction, body and conclusion are all evident, and the reader can locate material quickly.  Nevertheless, some headings are duplicated (two â6. Interpretation of Resultsâ) and appendices are poorly referenced, preventing a seamless flow.  This fits âwell organised with clear introduction, body and conclusion,â band 3.

Personal voice appears in the introduction and reflection, but the analysis itself is **not driven by an individual approach**âdata aggregation into six regions is explained, yet reasons for thresholds or grouping choices are generic.  Hence only âsome evidence of personal approach,â band 1.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated clearly and directly, providing focus and guiding the investigationâs scope.
The table of contents shows duplicated section headings and inconsistent referencing, interrupting the seamless flow between sections.
The introductionâs personal narrative is engaging but repeats ideas. Condense overlapping sentences to enhance conciseness and relevance.
Method names appear as âpersonsâ and âAvona.â Correct these to âPearsonâ and âANOVAâ for accurate mathematical terminology.
The variable table is well structured, clearly identifying independent and dependent variables with concise explanations.
Include units (%) in the headers of the regional data table to clarify the scale of the poverty and crime rates.
The hypotheses statements lack precision. Explicitly define Hâ: no correlation and Hâ: correlation exists between variables.
The scatter plot is effectively used to visualize trends, aiding in the interpretation of correlation assumptions.
The regression annotation omits RÂ². Including the coefficient of determination would strengthen your discussion of fit quality.
The Pearson formula is miswritten; the denominator should be \(\sqrt{\Sigma(x-\bar{x})^2\,\Sigma(y-\bar{y})^2}\), not mixing exponent positions.
Notation list uses âvâ for crime rate instead of the defined variable ây.â Maintain consistent symbols throughout.
The table of intermediate Pearson calculations is comprehensive but very wide; consider splitting it or rotating headers to improve readability.
Demonstrating substitution into the Pearson formula with actual sums shows strong use of mathematics.
Interpretation list mentions further testing but omits a p-value check here; add significance testing to substantiate claims.
The log-transformation table contains â0-621â which appears to be a typo. Verify all transformed values for accuracy.
Reporting of the reduced correlation (r=0.135) is clear; consider discussing whether the change is statistically significant.
The Spearman rank table effectively presents all steps; the structure supports transparent calculation.
The chi-square contingency table is clearly laid out but the binary categorization may reduce sensitivity; justify group thresholds or consider quartiles.
The expected frequency formula is clearly displayed and correctly applied, illustrating procedural rigor.
Degrees of freedom are correctly calculated as 1, ensuring validity of the chi-square test conclusions.
The ANOVA section lacks explicit sample sizes in the heading; clearly identify n for each group to support reproducibility.
The text describing the overall mean uses the term ânucleus,â which is unclear; revise for mathematical precision.
Use of a statistical library to compute the ANOVA p-value shows effective integration of technology in the analysis.
Link the numerical values of r and F directly in the interpretation to strengthen the connection between results and conclusions.
Reflection repeats the importance of confounding variables without proposing concrete changes; deepen critique by suggesting methodological enhancements.
Your personal motivation is clearly articulated in the introduction, demonstrating genuine engagement with the research question.
Group selection into six regions is generic; include statistical criteria or literature justification to show a more personal analytical approach.
Propose specific data sources or newer time frames in the limitations section to deepen the critical reflection.
Consider adding a process flowchart or schematic in the methodology to improve logical transitions and coherence.
